content.text: The shell clock ticks for everyone. The Tinkerer charges by the tick.

The Tinkerer owns the repair-and-modding lane — the shell clock's best friend. Armor comes back from breaks under a Tinkerer's hands, and mods go on at material cost instead of provider fees. The trade bleeds from Tech: the skill swings the tools; the profession is the client list that pays for them.

content.title: Tinkerer
content.subtitle: The repair lane

entries.label: Owns
entries.value: repair & modding — the shell clock's best friend

entries.label: Work verb
entries.value: repair and fabricate for credits (downtime, +2 PP)

entries.label: Field verb
entries.value: salvage via Scrap Eye (on mission, +1 PP)

content.desc: PP economy, tiers, and the One Name rule live on Professions Overview.

content.text: Perks — Sideline

Bench Rat (entry, 4 PP): opens the trade — repair and fabricate for credits and PP in downtime. In the field, patch armor for the squad at material cost +50% (see The Material Cost Law), with no permanent-consequence roll on a full-success Tech check.
Scrap Eye (4 PP): when the squad loots tech or weapons, the yield is +1 tier of salvage quality. This is the Tinkerer's field verb — exercising it on a mission earns PP.
Warranty Work (replaces Jury-Rig): your downtime repairs never trigger the permanent-consequence roll and restore +2 Armor HP per session.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Warranty Work's PP cost and tier are unattested. The amendments ledger replaces Jury-Rig (5 PP, Sideline) with Warranty Work — Jury-Rig's mid-fight shell repair moved to the Systems tree as Shell Splint — and Pass B confirms Warranty Work in the final tree, but the post-amendment Tinkerer tree document is not in the rebuild folder. The tree on this page is reconstructed from Professions v1 minus the deleted premises perks (The Shop, The Workshop — walls belong to the base), leaving six perks against the seven-to-eight of the other trades.
Perks — Established
Aftermarket License (T2, 6 PP): legal grey-zone mod catalog access one provider tier above your district (the catalog lives on Mods). Obligation: Pear audits, quarterly.
Client List (T2, 5 PP): your regulars feed you rumors — one free node Tag on any mission in your district (intel states — see Intel).
Perks — Name
Signature Work (T3, 8 PP): your mods carry your mark — +1 to one stat of any mod you install personally, and people know who built it. Obligation: that cuts both ways.

Signature Work and Warranty Work are different perks. Signature Work is the Name-tier mark on mods you install — the perk the Mods page points at. Warranty Work is the downtime repair perk that took Jury-Rig's slot when mid-fight repair moved to the Systems tree.
What the Tinkerer touches
Mods install at material cost instead of provider fees — Signature Work applies (see Mods).
Repair, reforge, and the break economy live on Armor; self-made and self-repaired work prices by The Material Cost Law.
Mid-fight shell repair is augment work, not trade work — Shell Splint lives on Systems. The profession keeps the economy; the tree gets the firefight.
Handloads: the Tinkerer presses a marksman's specialty rounds at material cost (see Precision and Consumables & Ammo).
Build choice: for a Systems operative the profession is a supply line — Tinkerer gear or Fixer intel (see Systems).
Pear Fabrication Services congratulates you on your registry. Your bench is now a matter of record. Quarterly audit dates are assigned, not requested.
they can audit the bench. they can't audit your hands. plan around that split.
